entry.title: MAD-HTLC
entry.summary: The concept of timelocked bribes emerges as a significant point in discussions about security mechanisms within digital transactions, specifically around the use of watchtowers. The essence of this idea is that the presence of timelocks may inherently allow the possibility of timelocked bribes. This correlation suggests that wherever a watchtower mechanism is proposed or implemented, the potential for these types of bribes exists. This insight underlines a key consideration in the design and implementation of cryptographic time-based functions, especially in how they can inadvertently open avenues for certain types of security breaches which must be anticipated and mitigated.
